## About this document

**Cross-platform** view of the canonical parquets consolidated in
`data/unified/`. Unlike the per-source qmds (`<plat>.qmd`) which describe
**one** platform, here the focus is on **comparing** platforms — stacked
bars by source, cross temporal distribution, cross models, capture_method,
global preservation.

The per-subset qmd setup defines `SOURCES_FILTER` (empty = all; list for a
subset like Web Chat / CLI / RAG).

```{python}
#| label: overview-totals

_rows = []
for table in ["conversations","messages","tool_events","branches",
              "sources","notes","outputs","guide_questions","source_guides",
              "project_metadata","project_docs"]:
    n = table_count(con, table)
    if n:
        _rows.append({"Table": f"{table}.parquet", "Rows": fmt_int(n)})
show_df(pd.DataFrame(_rows))
```

```{python}
#| label: overview-period

_period = con.execute("""
    SELECT MIN(created_at) AS oldest, MAX(created_at) AS newest, MAX(updated_at) AS last_update
    FROM conversations
""").df()
_oldest = _period["oldest"].iloc[0]
_newest = _period["newest"].iloc[0]
_last = _period["last_update"].iloc[0]

_meta_rows = [
    {"Field": "Subset", "Value": SUBSET_TITLE},
    {"Field": "Sources included", "Value": ", ".join(SOURCES_FILTER) if SOURCES_FILTER else "all (10)"},
    {"Field": "Creation period", "Value": f"{_oldest:%Y-%m-%d} → {_newest:%Y-%m-%d}" if pd.notna(_oldest) else "—"},
    {"Field": "Last update", "Value": f"{_last:%Y-%m-%d %H:%M}" if pd.notna(_last) else "—"},
]
show_df(pd.DataFrame(_meta_rows))
```

# 1. Platform × metrics comparison

Pivot table: each row is a platform, each column is a metric.
See "who has what" at a glance.

```{python}
#| label: comparative-table

_comp = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        source AS platform,
        COUNT(*) AS convs,
        SUM(message_count) AS msgs_total,
        ROUND(AVG(message_count), 1) AS avg_msgs,
        SUM(is_preserved_missing::INT) AS preserved,
        SUM(is_pinned::INT) AS pinned,
        MIN(created_at)::DATE AS oldest,
        MAX(created_at)::DATE AS newest
    FROM conversations
    GROUP BY source
    ORDER BY convs DESC
""").df()
show_df(_comp)
```

```{python}
#| label: tools-by-source

_tools = con.execute("""
    SELECT source AS platform,
        COUNT(*) AS tool_events,
        COUNT(DISTINCT tool_name) AS unique_tools,
        SUM(CASE WHEN success THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS success,
        SUM(CASE WHEN success = false THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS error
    FROM tool_events
    GROUP BY source
    ORDER BY tool_events DESC
""").df()
show_df(_tools)
```

# 2. Temporal distribution

## 2.1 Conversations created per month (stacked by platform)

```{python}
#| label: timeline-stacked

_tl = con.execute("""
    SELECT strftime(created_at, '%Y-%m') AS month,
        source AS platform,
        COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM conversations
    WHERE created_at IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY month, source
    ORDER BY month, source
""").df()

if len(_tl):
    import plotly.graph_objects as go
    pivot = _tl.pivot(index="month", columns="platform", values="n").fillna(0)
    fig = go.Figure()
    for col in pivot.columns:
        fig.add_trace(go.Bar(x=pivot.index, y=pivot[col],
                              name=col, marker_color=SOURCE_COLORS.get(col, MUTED)))
    fig.update_layout(
        barmode="stack", title="Conversations created per month (stacked by platform)",
        height=400, template="plotly_white",
        xaxis=dict(type="category"),
        margin=dict(t=50, b=80, l=40, r=20),
        legend=dict(orientation="h", y=-0.25),
    )
    fig.show()
```

## 2.2 Cumulative growth by platform

```{python}
#| label: cumulative-by-source

_cum = con.execute("""
    SELECT strftime(created_at, '%Y-%m') AS month,
        source AS platform,
        COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM conversations
    WHERE created_at IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY month, source
    ORDER BY month, source
""").df()

if len(_cum):
    import plotly.graph_objects as go
    fig = go.Figure()
    for src in _cum["platform"].unique():
        sub = _cum[_cum["platform"] == src].sort_values("month")
        cum = sub["n"].cumsum()
        fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            x=sub["month"], y=cum, mode="lines+markers", name=src,
            line=dict(color=SOURCE_COLORS.get(src, MUTED), width=2),
        ))
    fig.update_layout(
        title="Cumulative growth by platform",
        height=400, template="plotly_white",
        xaxis=dict(type="category", title="Month"),
        yaxis=dict(title="Cumulative convs"),
        margin=dict(t=50, b=40, l=40, r=20),
    )
    fig.show()
```

## 2.3 Activity heatmap (hour × day, all sources)

```{python}
#| label: heatmap-cross

_hm = con.execute("""
    SELECT EXTRACT(isodow FROM created_at) AS dow,
        EXTRACT(hour FROM created_at) AS hour,
        COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM conversations WHERE created_at IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY dow, hour
""").df()

if len(_hm):
    import plotly.graph_objects as go
    pivot = _hm.pivot(index="dow", columns="hour", values="n").fillna(0)
    pivot = pivot.reindex(range(1, 8))
    days = ["Mon","Tue","Wed","Thu","Fri","Sat","Sun"]
    fig = go.Figure(go.Heatmap(
        z=pivot.values, x=list(pivot.columns), y=days,
        colorscale=[[0, "#ffffff"], [1, "#1f2937"]],
    ))
    fig.update_layout(
        title="Activity — hour × day (all platforms)",
        height=300, template="plotly_white",
        xaxis=dict(title="Hour", dtick=2),
        margin=dict(t=50, b=40, l=60, r=20),
    )
    fig.show()
```

# 3. Cross-platform models

## 3.1 Top 20 models (with source badge)

```{python}
#| label: top-models-cross

_tm = con.execute("""
    SELECT model, source AS platform, COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM conversations WHERE model IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY model, source
    ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 20
""").df()
show_df(_tm)
```

## 3.2 Model × platform heatmap (top 30 models)

```{python}
#| label: model-platform-heat

_mph = con.execute("""
    WITH top_models AS (
        SELECT model FROM conversations WHERE model IS NOT NULL
        GROUP BY model ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 30
    )
    SELECT model, source AS platform, COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM conversations
    WHERE model IN (SELECT model FROM top_models)
    GROUP BY model, source
""").df()

if len(_mph):
    import plotly.graph_objects as go
    pivot = _mph.pivot(index="model", columns="platform", values="n").fillna(0)
    fig = go.Figure(go.Heatmap(
        z=pivot.values, x=list(pivot.columns), y=list(pivot.index),
        colorscale=[[0, "#ffffff"], [1, "#1f2937"]],
        text=pivot.values, texttemplate="%{text:.0f}",
    ))
    fig.update_layout(
        title="Usage distribution: model × platform",
        height=600, template="plotly_white",
        margin=dict(t=50, b=40, l=140, r=20),
    )
    fig.show()
```

# 4. Capture method (data provenance)

Distinguishes data captured by the automatic extractor vs imported via manual
parsers (clippings_obsidian, copypaste_web, terminal_claude_code,
legacy_notebooklm).

```{python}
#| label: capture-method-cross

if has_col(con, "conversations", "capture_method"):
    _cm = con.execute("""
        SELECT
            COALESCE(capture_method, 'extractor') AS method,
            source AS platform,
            COUNT(*) AS n
        FROM conversations
        GROUP BY method, source
        ORDER BY method, n DESC
    """).df()
    show_df(_cm)

    import plotly.graph_objects as go
    pivot = _cm.pivot(index="method", columns="platform", values="n").fillna(0)
    fig = go.Figure()
    for col in pivot.columns:
        fig.add_trace(go.Bar(x=pivot.index, y=pivot[col],
                              name=col, marker_color=SOURCE_COLORS.get(col, MUTED)))
    fig.update_layout(
        barmode="stack", title="Conversations by capture_method (stacked by platform)",
        height=400, template="plotly_white",
        margin=dict(t=50, b=80, l=40, r=20),
        legend=dict(orientation="h", y=-0.25),
    )
    fig.show()
else:
    show_md("> `capture_method` absent in the views (all extractor).")
```

# 5. Cross-platform tool events

## 5.1 Top 20 tools used

```{python}
#| label: top-tools-cross

if table_count(con, "tool_events") > 0:
    _tt = con.execute("""
        SELECT tool_name, source AS platform, COUNT(*) AS calls
        FROM tool_events
        WHERE tool_name IS NOT NULL AND tool_name != ''
        GROUP BY tool_name, source
        ORDER BY calls DESC LIMIT 20
    """).df()
    show_df(_tt)
```

## 5.2 Tools by category (event_type) cross

```{python}
#| label: events-by-type-cross

if table_count(con, "tool_events") > 0:
    _et = con.execute("""
        SELECT event_type, source AS platform, COUNT(*) AS n
        FROM tool_events
        GROUP BY event_type, source
        ORDER BY n DESC
    """).df()

    import plotly.graph_objects as go
    pivot = _et.pivot(index="event_type", columns="platform", values="n").fillna(0)
    fig = go.Figure()
    for col in pivot.columns:
        fig.add_trace(go.Bar(x=pivot.index, y=pivot[col],
                              name=col, marker_color=SOURCE_COLORS.get(col, MUTED)))
    fig.update_layout(
        barmode="stack", title="ToolEvents by event_type (stacked by platform)",
        height=400, template="plotly_white",
        margin=dict(t=50, b=80, l=40, r=20),
        legend=dict(orientation="h", y=-0.25),
    )
    fig.show()
```

## 5.3 Success rate (CLIs)

```{python}
#| label: success-rate-cross

if has_col(con, "tool_events", "success"):
    _sr = con.execute("""
        SELECT source AS platform,
            SUM(CASE WHEN success THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS success,
            SUM(CASE WHEN success = false THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS error,
            SUM(CASE WHEN success IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS unknown,
            COUNT(*) AS total
        FROM tool_events
        GROUP BY source
        HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN success IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0
        ORDER BY total DESC
    """).df()
    if len(_sr):
        show_df(_sr)
    else:
        show_md("> No platform in this subset records success on tool_events.")
```

# 6. Cross-platform word counts

```{python}
#| label: words-cross

_wc = con.execute("""
    SELECT source AS platform, role,
        ROUND(AVG(word_count), 0)::INT AS avg_words,
        MEDIAN(word_count)::INT AS median_words,
        MAX(word_count) AS max_words,
        SUM(word_count) AS total_words
    FROM messages
    WHERE NOT is_hidden AND word_count > 0
    GROUP BY source, role
    ORDER BY source, role
""").df()
show_df(_wc)
```

# 7. Preservation and special states

## 7.1 Preserved missing global

Convs active on the server vs preserved locally (disappeared from server).

```{python}
#| label: preservation-cross

_pres = con.execute("""
    SELECT source AS platform,
        SUM(is_preserved_missing::INT) AS preserved,
        COUNT(*) AS total,
        ROUND(SUM(is_preserved_missing::INT) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0), 1) AS pct
    FROM conversations
    GROUP BY source
    HAVING SUM(is_preserved_missing::INT) > 0
    ORDER BY preserved DESC
""").df()
if len(_pres):
    show_df(_pres)
else:
    show_md("> Zero preserved convs (deleted from server) in this subset.")
```

## 7.2 Pinned / Archived / Temporary

```{python}
#| label: states-cross

_st = con.execute("""
    SELECT source AS platform,
        SUM(is_pinned::INT) AS pinned,
        SUM(is_archived::INT) AS archived,
        SUM(is_temporary::INT) AS temporary,
        COUNT(*) AS total
    FROM conversations
    GROUP BY source
    HAVING SUM(is_pinned::INT) + SUM(is_archived::INT) + SUM(is_temporary::INT) > 0
    ORDER BY pinned DESC, archived DESC
""").df()
if len(_st):
    show_df(_st)
else:
    show_md("> No platform in this subset records pin/archive/temporary.")
```

# 8. Conversation lifetime

`updated_at - created_at`. Distinguishes one-shot (< 1 day) from threads
that became a project (> 7 days).

```{python}
#| label: lifetime-cross

_lf = con.execute("""
    WITH lt AS (
        SELECT source, EXTRACT(epoch FROM updated_at - created_at) / 86400 AS days
        FROM conversations
        WHERE updated_at IS NOT NULL AND created_at IS NOT NULL
    )
    SELECT source AS platform,
        CASE
            WHEN days < 0.04167 THEN '< 1h'
            WHEN days < 1 THEN '1h-1d'
            WHEN days < 7 THEN '1-7 days'
            WHEN days < 30 THEN '7-30 days'
            WHEN days < 90 THEN '30-90 days'
            ELSE '> 90 days'
        END AS bin,
        COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM lt GROUP BY source, bin
""").df()

if len(_lf):
    _order = ['< 1h','1h-1d','1-7 days','7-30 days','30-90 days','> 90 days']
    _lf['bin'] = pd.Categorical(_lf['bin'], categories=_order, ordered=True)
    _lf = _lf.sort_values(['bin', 'platform'])

    import plotly.graph_objects as go
    pivot = _lf.pivot(index="bin", columns="platform", values="n").fillna(0)
    pivot = pivot.reindex(_order)
    fig = go.Figure()
    for col in pivot.columns:
        fig.add_trace(go.Bar(x=pivot.index.astype(str), y=pivot[col],
                              name=col, marker_color=SOURCE_COLORS.get(col, MUTED)))
    fig.update_layout(
        barmode="stack", title="Conversation lifetime (stacked by platform)",
        height=400, template="plotly_white",
        margin=dict(t=50, b=80, l=40, r=20),
        legend=dict(orientation="h", y=-0.25),
    )
    fig.show()
```

# 9. Full filterable cross-platform table

```{python}
#| label: full-table-cross
#| column: page

from itables import show as itable_show

_full = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        source AS platform,
        substr(conversation_id, 1, 8) AS conv_id,
        title,
        message_count AS msgs,
        model,
        project,
        strftime(created_at, '%Y-%m-%d') AS created,
        is_preserved_missing AS preserved,
        is_pinned AS pinned
    FROM conversations
    ORDER BY created_at DESC
""").df()

itable_show(
    _full,
    paging=True, searching=True, ordering=True, info=True,
    lengthMenu=[10, 25, 50, 100],
    classes="display compact",
)
```
